reasoning: In the March 2025 Greenlandic general election, the moderate Demokraatit party secured a significant victory. As of April 2025, Jens-Frederik Nielsen leads the new coalition government. He and his coalition have explicitly adopted a "cautious approach" to independence, emphasizing economic development over urgent constitutional changes, and Nielsen has publicly stated that his party "does not set a date" for independence. The pro-referendum party, Naleraq, was excluded from the governing coalition. Given this clear political stance from the newly formed government and the extremely short timeline (less than two months) between the forecast date (May 2, 2025) and the resolution date (July 1, 2025), it is practically impossible for the government to suddenly reverse course and officially schedule an independence referendum.
